title: Exciting Releases: TVS Jupiter CNG, BMW F 450 GS & Aprilia Tuareg 457
description: Late April 2026 is set to shake up the Indian two-wheeler landscape, introducing the country’s initial CNG scooter and a thrilling mid-capacity adventure bike rivalry.
content: The latter half of April 2026 looks promising for the Indian two-wheeler market, with significant launches poised to transform both the commuter and premium adventure segments.

Kicking off on April 15 is the eagerly awaited TVS Jupiter CNG. As India’s premier CNG-powered scooter, it aims to change the game for daily commuting in urban areas by incorporating dual-fuel technology and offering dramatically reduced operating costs.

Adventure lovers can look forward to April 30 for an exhilarating off-road contest. BMW Motorrad will unveil the F 450 GS, a robust, mid-weight ADV crafted to bridge the space between the entry-level 310 GS and the heavyweight 900 GS seamlessly.

On the same date, Aprilia will introduce the Tuareg 457. Powered by the dynamic twin-cylinder engine from the RS 457, this rally-inspired adventure bike is geared up to compete directly with the Royal Enfield Himalayan 450.
Whether you’re seeking affordable daily travel or thrilling weekend trail adventures, the upcoming weeks are bound to present something extraordinary for Indian biking enthusiasts.
pros: TVS Jupiter CNG delivers unmatched low operating expenses
BMW F 450 GS features true premium GS technology on a lighter frame
Aprilia Tuareg 457 boasts high-performance twin-cylinder power
Wide range of options catering to various budget needs
cons: CNG refueling stations remain scarce in Tier-3 cities
Aprilia and BMW models may come with a premium due to import/brand taxes
Higher seat heights on the ADVs may not suit shorter riders

title: New Bike Launches April 2026: Triumph, Bajaj, and RE Electric
description: April 2026 marks a massive shift in India's two-wheeler market with affordable Triumph 350s, the Pulsar 180 comeback, and Royal Enfield's first EV.
content: Triumph India is officially launching its 350cc lineup on April 6, 2026, including the Speed T4 350 and Scrambler 350 X.
The 2026 Bajaj Pulsar 180 is making a surprise comeback with a new LED headlamp, digital LCD console, and Bluetooth navigation.
Royal Enfield is set to unveil the production version of the 'Flying Flea C6' electric bike by the end of April.
These new models target the growing demand for mid-capacity performance and premium electric urban mobility in India.
With aggressive pricing and modern tech, these launches are expected to dominate the market sentiment this quarter.
pros: Modern tech updates for the iconic Pulsar 180
Unique retro-modern design of RE Flying Flea
cons: RE Electric may have a high entry price
Triumph 350 offers lower power than the 400 series

title: Ather Affordable Electric Scooter 2026: Price Will Not Be Below ₹1 Lakh
description: Ather Energy’s upcoming affordable electric scooter in 2026 is expected to be priced above ₹1 lakh. Check expected features, range, and market impact.
content: is planning to launch a new affordable electric scooter in India, but recent updates confirm that its price will not fall below ₹1 lakh.
This upcoming model is expected to target buyers looking for a premium yet slightly affordable electric scooter with strong performance and modern features.
Ather continues to focus on quality, smart technology, and riding experience rather than entering the ultra-budget EV segment.
The scooter is likely to offer improved battery efficiency, connected features, and a refined riding experience suitable for both city and daily commuting.
With this pricing strategy, Ather aims to compete with brands like  and  in the mid-range EV market.
cons: Price higher than expected entry-level EVs

title: Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 2026: Refreshed Model Announced
description: The 2026 Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 has been announced with a refreshed lineup, sharper styling, and improved highway performance in the mid-size motorcycle segment.
content: The 2026 Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 has been announced, and it brings a refreshed lineup of models, sharper styling, and better highway behavior to the mid-size motorcycle segment.
This refreshed version of the Guerrilla 450 continues to offer the sporty roadster style that users have come to love, but now with more options and styling changes to improve road presence.
The focus of this motorcycle is to provide a solid mid-range engine, offering versatility for both city riding and highway use.
cons: A bit heavy for new riders

title: Hero Splendor City Ride: Mileage King
description: Splendor remains a commuter favorite for its fuel economy.
content: Hero Splendor continues to be the commuter benchmark with excellent mileage.
The seating position is easy and upright, and the bike feels light in traffic.
For daily city use with low running cost, it is still a dependable choice.

title: Bajaj Pulsar NS200 Review: Street Sprint
description: A performance commuter with sharp handling and punchy power.
content: Bajaj Pulsar NS200 brings performance to the daily commute.
The motor delivers a strong mid-range, and the chassis feels stable at speed.
It suits riders who want fun with practical everyday usability.

title: TVS Apache RR310 Review: Track Inspired
description: A full-fairing bike with sporty ergonomics and strong braking.
content: TVS Apache RR310 delivers a sporty experience with confident braking.
The riding posture feels focused, and the bike stays stable at higher speeds.
It is ideal for riders who want weekend performance with premium styling.
